position_summary: Claude's position is directionally defensible but too generous: the draft has the right distinctive claims, yet several sentences still let the method inherit more experimental, follow-up, or independence weight than the evidence supports. The two-receipt distinction is explicitly stated in places, but hostile readers will attack the surrounding verbs and shorthand labels, especially 'supported chain,' 'true in fresh experimental data,' and claims about self-audit and UI automation.

findings.title: The core 'follow-up' problem is not fully closed by retrospective adjudication
findings.evidence: Introduction lines 36-41 frame the missing step as asking whether each hypothesis is 'actually supported by data'; Background lines 28-31 contrasts time-slicing with whether a hypothesis is 'true in fresh experimental data.' But Section 2.2 lines 38-42 correctly says the referee queries pre-existing measurements retrospectively, not commissioned follow-up.
findings.concern_path: A hostile LBD reviewer will say the paper uses the R01 critique about hypotheses not being followed up, then substitutes retrospective lookup in a held dataset for actual follow-up. That is a valid triage contribution, but the prose sometimes sounds like it solves experimental follow-up rather than prioritizes it.
findings.suggested_action: Keep the triage-loop claim sharp, but explicitly say the method closes the triage/adjudication loop, not the experimental-follow-up loop. Replace 'true in fresh experimental data' with language about independent, pre-existing experimental substrate or held perturbation data.

findings.title: The manuscript still leaks discovery language at the opening
findings.evidence: Introduction line 17 says 'where new discoveries wait, hidden in plain sight'; line 23 uses 'candidate discovery.' The user explicitly wants calibrated language and no unearned 'discovered/proven' framing.
findings.concern_path: The opening paragraph sets reviewer expectations before the receipt caveat arrives. Even if later text says nomination, an FRMA reviewer can quote the first paragraph as overclaiming that the system finds discoveries, not nominations or re-derived candidate chains.
findings.suggested_action: Replace 'new discoveries wait' with 'candidate connections' or 'testable nominations.' 'Candidate discovery' is acceptable in classic LBD context only if immediately bounded as literature novelty, not biological truth.

findings.title: Two-receipt distinction is stated, but later shorthand blurs it
findings.evidence: Introduction lines 46-50 correctly distinguishes experimental versus association receipts. But lines 63-69 say 'receipt-backed survivors,' 'support it as a Th1/Th2 regulator,' 'atopic-eczema link,' and 'including a falsification of its sharpest confounder' in one sentence. Methods line 84 asks whether the downstream signature 'fall[s] in a module enriched for the specific disease' but does not restate GWAS/association limitations at the hop definition.
findings.concern_path: A skeptical biology reviewer will read the full chain as experimentally supported unless every compressed reference to the disease hop repeats that it is an association nomination. The confounder falsification sentence is especially risky: falsifying STAT6 cis bleed strengthens NAB2-specific perturbation, but it does not experimentally validate the disease association.
findings.suggested_action: Whenever 'full chain,' 'supported,' or 'disease-supported' appears near the disease hop, append a compact qualifier such as 'with the disease hop remaining GWAS-association evidence.' Make the STAT6 confounder language say it falsifies a cis-artifact explanation of the perturbation signal, not the disease nomination itself.

findings.title: Open Targets novelty score is described as genetic-association evidence, but the code uses general associatedTargets scores
findings.evidence: Methods lines 30-32 says 'curated gene→disease genetic-association scores come from the Open Targets GraphQL API'; lines 50-52 call ac_known the 'Open Targets genetic-association score.' In src/arbiter/lbd/sources.py, the query reads disease.associatedTargets rows with target.approvedSymbol and score; it does not select a genetics-only datasource or evidence type.
findings.concern_path: A reviewer familiar with Open Targets will object that the overall association score is not automatically a genetic-association score. This matters because the manuscript uses ac_known as a novelty/known-genetic-link gate, while the disease receipt class is separately GWAS-genetic from the perturbation study's T3 labels.
findings.suggested_action: Separate the two concepts: T3 disease labels are genetic-association/module-enrichment receipts inherited from the source study; ac_known is an Open Targets target-disease association score used as a known-link/novelty prior unless the code truly filters to genetic evidence.

findings.title: The referee's 'confident no' is overstated because most funnel hops are preselected
findings.evidence: Methods lines 38-45 define A by knockdown QC, transcriptional effect, and Th1/Th2 significance before disease testing. Methods lines 91-96 admits only the program hop is tautological. src/arbiter/lbd/entities.py confirms A requires KD gate plus effect, and optionally program_significant; src/arbiter/lbd/propose.py then runs the referee after eligibility.
findings.concern_path: Claude defends the confident NO as a moat, but within the main funnel Hop 0, much of Hop 1, and Hop 2 have already been selected for. The dominant cull is exact disease-C support, with only 1 refuted-effect among eligible pairs in the stage1 artifact. A hostile reviewer will say the no is mostly a disease-membership lookup after prefiltering, not a broad mechanistic falsifier.
findings.suggested_action: Expand the honesty sentence in 3.3: within the generated funnel, KD and program are largely pre-gated, effect is partly pre-gated, and the large cull is disease-C specificity; the confident-no behavior is demonstrated by demotions and exact-C refutations, not by independent discrimination at all four hops among funnel survivors.

findings.title: 'Supported' terminology remains too strong for association-hop chains
findings.evidence: Methods lines 88-94 defines 'supported' for full chain and 'refuted-for-C'; stage1/sweep_Stim8hr.json uses referee_overall strings saying 'consistent with a validated gene -> program -> disease chain re-derived from the tables.' The manuscript says language was calibrated, but the primary artifact still contains 'validated' in referee_overall fields.
findings.concern_path: Even if manuscript prose avoids 'validated,' the artifacts a reviewer inspects still say 'validated gene -> program -> disease chain.' That directly contradicts the paper's calibration claim and will make the self-audit look incomplete or cosmetic.
findings.suggested_action: Do not claim the platform removed overstated language from all output unless the emitted artifacts are also cleaned or clearly scoped. In the manuscript, define 'supported' as a verdict label with bounded semantics, and acknowledge legacy artifact wording if artifacts are not regenerated.

findings.title: Self-audit independence is carefully bounded, but 'The platform checked its own work' is rhetorically vulnerable
findings.evidence: Introduction lines 56-60 culminates in 'The platform checked its own work.' Background lines 72-78 gives the better qualified version: role, model, and checkpoint independence within one model family, with cross-vendor replication external.
findings.concern_path: A hostile reviewer will attack 'platform checked its own work' as circular, especially because both actor and critic are Anthropic-family models inside Claude Science. The qualified version survives; the slogan does not.
findings.suggested_action: Keep the independent critic-model claim, but avoid the slogan or immediately attach the boundary: role/model/checkpoint independence within one family, with cross-family replication external.

findings.title: No-API headless automation is distinctive but currently reads as security-bypassing rather than reproducibility engineering
findings.evidence: Methods lines 106-117 says the driver loads an authenticated session and auto-approves working-directory, code-execution, and network-access prompts for unattended operation, then calls this 'principled UI automation' and 'the honest substitute.'
findings.concern_path: This is a novel methods element worth preserving, but the current prose invites a reviewer to ask whether the method bypasses user-consent and sandbox controls. 'Auto-approves network/code prompts' sounds like disabling safety friction, not a reproducible instrument protocol.
findings.suggested_action: Keep the no-API/headless UI automation claim, but add the missing guardrails: fixed workspace, saved authenticated session owned by the operator, logged prompt approvals, bounded network/cache modes, and why unattended approval did not alter data receipts. Replace 'honest substitute' with less moralized engineering language.

findings.title: The grant identifier appears despite the stated author decision not to name it
findings.evidence: Background line 28 names '(NIH 1R01LM015392-01).' The prompt states the grant number is deliberately not named and should not be treated as an omission.
findings.concern_path: This is not just a style issue. Naming an unfunded proposal identifier can create unnecessary reviewer distraction and conflicts with the author decision supplied for the debate.
findings.suggested_action: Remove the grant number from manuscript prose while retaining the verbatim quote and 'proposal submitted for funding consideration' framing.

findings.title: The Background under-motivates why a held Perturb-seq substrate is the right alternative to time-slicing
findings.evidence: Background lines 28-31 says standard evaluations do not test truth in fresh data; Section 2.2 lines 36-45 introduces the held substrate. The transition asserts the need but does not explain why this substrate is a fair or limited adjudicator for LBD hypotheses.
findings.concern_path: FRMA/LBD readers may ask why the right evaluation is a single domain-specific T-cell Perturb-seq substrate rather than time-slicing, prospective literature emergence, expert curation, or external experimental follow-up. Without a stronger bridge, the paper can look like it evaluates only hypotheses that the chosen substrate happens to be able to answer.
findings.suggested_action: Add a reviewer-proofing sentence: the substrate is not a universal truth oracle; it is a bounded, independent adjudication surface for mechanistic T-cell claims, useful precisely because it can return support/refutation/untested with receipts.

findings.title: The 'answer-free' universe is defensible but may sound stronger than it is
findings.evidence: Methods lines 36-43 says A is defined before disease information and cannot be contaminated by the answer. That is true for T3 disease labels, but A is selected on the same program and effect evidence later used by the referee.
findings.concern_path: A reviewer can agree A is disease-answer-free while still objecting that it is not referee-evidence-free. This matters because the prose uses answer-free to imply independence, while the referee later reuses much of the same evidence.
findings.suggested_action: Say 'disease-answer-free' or 'T3-free' rather than broad 'answer-free' when discussing A. Preserve the point that disease labels are not consulted, but do not imply independence from all later hop receipts.

findings.title: The methods do not justify the ranking objective enough for a metrics audience
findings.evidence: Methods lines 57-70 gives the formula and says it was designed offline, with its structure fixed before running. It does not explain sensitivity, alternatives, or why the chosen weights beta=1, w=1, w2=3 are not tuned to NAB2.
findings.concern_path: For FRMA, the ranking objective is part of the method, not a harmless implementation detail. A reviewer will ask whether weights were chosen after seeing the survivor list, whether rank stability matters, and whether 'only human judgment' is too casual for an objective that determines the worked example's prominence.
findings.suggested_action: Do not soften the objective away; instead pre-empt with provenance and sensitivity language in Results or Methods: fixed-before-run evidence, rank stability under plausible weights, and clear separation of eligibility/referee verdict from ranking priority.

findings.title: The direct-literature novelty story is exposed by nonzero A-C co-mentions
findings.evidence: Introduction line 62 calls NAB2 the 'highest-ranked near-novel survivor'; the artifact reports NAB2-atopic eczema ac_lit=6 and Open Targets ac_known=0.0376. Introduction line 23 explains classic LBD as an absent A-C edge candidate discovery.
findings.concern_path: The draft mostly uses 'near-novel,' but the classic LBD setup primes readers for missing A-C edges. A hostile reviewer may say NAB2 is not Swanson-style undiscovered because it has direct co-mentions and a nonzero association score.
findings.suggested_action: Make the novelty criterion explicit before NAB2 appears: this is not strict A-C absence; it is low direct-literature plus low curated association under a bridge-and-effect objective. Preserve 'near-novel,' avoid 'missing edge' as the operational criterion for this implementation.

findings.title: Negative-control language is too compressed to pre-empt artifact concerns
findings.evidence: Introduction lines 63-66 says a negative-control panel shows discrimination is not an artifact of the setup, but Sections 1-3 do not state what the negative controls are or what artifact they address.
findings.concern_path: A reviewer will not wait until Section 4 if the Introduction makes a strong anti-artifact claim. Without one clause explaining the control class, the claim reads like a generic reassurance.
findings.suggested_action: Add a compact identifier for the negative-control panel, for example non-immune disease controls or whatever Section 4 uses, and state the artifact class it tests.

convergence_status: round 1 — no prior position to converge with
recommended_next_question: Claude should address whether the draft can explicitly separate triage from experimental follow-up, restrict 'supported' to bounded verdict semantics, and fix the Open Targets/genetic-association misdescription without sanding off the agentic workbench, confident-NO, critic-model, or no-API automation claims.
